items.text:
Introduction to Biology — Lab Component Guidelines (Spring Semester)

The laboratory portion of BIOL 101 contributes 35% of the final course grade and is required for all enrolled students. Weekly lab reports account for 20%, two practical exams contribute another 10%, and lab attendance and preparation make up the remaining 5%.

All lab reports must be submitted through the online portal by 11:59 PM on the Sunday following each scheduled lab. Late reports are accepted within 48 hours of the deadline for a 15% penalty. Reports submitted after 48 hours will not receive credit.

A non-refundable safety equipment fee of $25 must be paid by January 22 at the Bursar's Office in Anderson Hall. The fee covers safety goggles, lab notebooks, and access to the microscope library. Students who miss more than three lab sessions without an excused absence will be required to repeat the course.

Please contact your TA, Ms. Lin, in Room 204 during her Tuesday office hours (2:00-4:00 PM) for any questions about grading or makeup labs.

questions.stem: According to the syllabus, what percentage of the course grade comes from lab attendance and preparation?
questions.explanation: The syllabus states that attendance and preparation make up the remaining 5%.

questions.stem: According to the syllabus, what must students do by January 22?

options.A: Submit the first weekly lab report
options.B: Pay the $25 non-refundable safety equipment fee
options.C: Reserve a microscope at Anderson Hall
options.D: Register for a makeup lab with the TA

questions.explanation: The syllabus says the $25 safety fee must be paid by January 22 at the Bursar's Office.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who submits a lab report 60 hours after the deadline?

options.A: The student will be charged an additional safety fee.
options.B: The student will not receive any credit for that report.
options.C: The student will lose only 15% of the report's value.
options.D: The student will need to repeat the entire course.

questions.explanation: Reports submitted more than 48 hours after the deadline will not receive credit.

items.text:
Wi-Fi Security Certificate Update — March 18

The campus wireless network certificate will be updated on March 18 between 6:00 AM and 8:00 AM. After the update, all devices currently connected to CampusNet must reconfigure their secure connection before regaining access.

Details of the update:

- Affected service: CampusNet secure wireless
- Outage window: 6:00 AM - 8:00 AM, March 18
- Devices impacted: laptops, smartphones, tablets, and printers
- Drop-in support: Library Help Desk, 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, March 18-19
- Self-service guide: available on the IT portal

A $10 device-setup support fee will be charged for technicians making in-room visits to faculty offices, though walk-in help at the Library Help Desk remains free for all students. Devices that fail to update after March 20 may be temporarily blocked from network access.

Questions may be directed to the Network Operations Center at netops@example.edu or to Mr. Kowalski at extension 4582. Students are encouraged to update before the weekend to avoid service interruptions during midterm week.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when can students get drop-in help at the Library Help Desk?

options.A: Between 6:00 AM and 8:00 AM on March 18
options.B: From midnight to noon on March 19 only
options.C: From 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM on March 18 and 19
options.D: From 4:00 PM to 8:00 PM on March 20 only

questions.explanation: The notice lists Library Help Desk drop-in support as 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM on March 18-19.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a faculty member who needs in-room support?

options.A: The faculty member will not be charged any fees for any visit.
options.B: The faculty member must travel to the Network Operations Center.
options.C: The faculty member is required to file a written request in advance.
options.D: The faculty member must pay the $10 device-setup fee.

questions.explanation: The notice states that in-room visits to faculty offices carry a $10 device-setup support fee.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To advertise new wireless features available on campus.
options.B: To inform users about a certificate update and steps to keep network access.
options.C: To announce a permanent change to the campus internet provider.
options.D: To recruit student volunteers for help-desk technician roles.

questions.explanation: The notice introduces the certificate update, lists the outage window, and describes how to regain access.
